source.title: The hippocampus as a predictive map

confidence_rationale: Influential computational paper proposing that hippocampal place-cell representations encode a successor representation -- a predictive summary of future state-occupancy. This is one candidate implementation of bottom-up rule discovery: rules-as-summary-statistics over the trajectory record. Strong on the architectural hypothesis but the empirical evidence is post-hoc reinterpretation of existing place-cell data, not a forward prediction.
failure_signatures: An REE substrate that does not compute predictive (successor-style) summaries of trajectory statistics will fail to reproduce the place-cell-tuning-asymmetry signatures Stachenfeld 2017 reports -- specifically, asymmetric tuning toward upcoming locations in the agent's typical path.

If REE's ARC-064 implementation does not converge on something successor-representation-shaped from the trajectory record, the rule-discovery is not capturing predictive statistics, only descriptive ones; the bottom-up pathway would then be a clustering rather than a planning substrate.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal place cells encode not a geometric map of space but a predictive map: each location's representation summarises future occupancy probabilities under the current policy. This account explains place-cell asymmetries (tuning shifted toward upcoming locations along the typical path), policy dependence (place fields shift when the policy changes), and grid-cell properties (entorhinal grid cells encode a low-dimensional basis for the predictive representation, useful for noise suppression and multi-scale planning).
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-064 (bottom-up rule discovery) could be implemented as a successor-representation-style summary over the trajectory record: each visited state gets a prediction of which states typically follow under the agent's current policy, and rule-equivalents emerge as patterns in this predictive structure. This would be one specific implementation choice among the candidates Niv 2019 lists; the Stachenfeld paper makes the case that the brain has already converged on this implementation. Cross-reference: existing REE substrates MECH-269 (anchor sets) and MECH-292 (ranked ghost-goal bank) are episode-anchored rather than predictive-summary; an SR-style ARC-064 substrate would be architecturally distinct.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Stachenfeld et al. is primarily a theoretical reinterpretation of existing place-cell data, not a primary forward-tested empirical paper. The mapping is post-hoc -- they show that an SR account can reproduce known place-cell phenomena. The mapping to ARC-064's behavioural-pattern-extraction is conceptually clean but requires choosing the right granularity for the predictive statistics (over states? over actions? over trajectory chunks?). Transfer risk moderate -- the framework is computationally tractable and biologically plausible, but the implementation parameter choices are not pinned down by the paper.
mapping.source_context: Nat Neurosci computational paper from DeepMind + Princeton + Harvard. Stachenfeld is the first author; Gershman has been the primary advocate of the SR framework in cognitive neuroscience.

confidence_components.notes: Nat Neurosci venue and influential framework. Mapping fidelity moderate -- the SR is one specific implementation candidate, not 'the' answer. Transfer risk slightly elevated because the empirical evidence is post-hoc reinterpretation rather than primary data, and the implementation granularity choices are open.
